_doc: The contract between a lens scanner subagent and the review cache. Each scanner RETURNS one object of this shape and it is written to .deai/review/<unit>__<lensId>.json. collate.mjs merges every such file into .deai/review.json. The /review page reads that and POSTs each finding's triage `decision` to .deai/review-decisions.json (keyed by finding id). NO field here ever causes a Drive write — the review engine is diagnostic; queued findings flow to the apply passes named by `route`. READABILITY IS THE CONTRACT: `why` is one punch. A finding is shown as a TRACK-CHANGES REDLINE: the diff between `original` (verbatim before) and `edited` (after). The op (cut/replace/add) is DERIVED from that diff, never stored — so a cut always reads as a cut. A structural finding with no single-sentence edit stores `action` (a short instruction) instead of original/edited.

lensFile.description: the craft KB file the scanner loaded, e.g. 'craft/pacing/pacing.md'

unit.description: the review unit scanned, e.g. 'ch07' or 'p41-48'

findings.description: Weaknesses/opportunities found. Empty array is a valid, good result — a clean unit.

items.description: Each finding MUST carry EITHER (original AND edited) for a concrete sentence-level edit, OR (action) for a structural change with no single-sentence diff. Never both, never neither.

scope.description: line = a sentence-level edit on a specific page (original/edited); structural = chapter/book-level (action)

intent.description: fix = corrects a genuine weakness/defect. enhance = an ENHANCEMENT OPPORTUNITY: the passage is already fine but a specific named technique from the craft file would make it land harder (constructive-mode lenses only). Reductive lenses always emit 'fix'. The /review page badges 'enhance' findings so the author can see where the research ELEVATES the work, not just where it corrects.

principle.description: the NAMED technique/rule from the lens file this finding is judged against. A short LABEL, not a sentence.

source.description: provenance of the principle — cite the research, not training. One short tag.

page.description: 1-based page (pages.sh numbering); null for book-level structural findings.

severity.description: how much this hurts the book.

confidence.description: 0 hunch · 1 plausible · 2 solid · 3 certain. Bias low when leaning on taste.

why.description: ONE punch. The reason it's a problem, ≤ ~20 words, no preamble, no second sentence.

original.description: LINE EDIT ONLY: the EXACT verbatim sentence(s) from the manuscript that the edit changes — char-for-char, curly quotes intact, from the start of the first affected sentence to the end of the last. This is the 'before'. Must be findable verbatim in manuscript.txt. Omit for structural.

edited.description: LINE EDIT ONLY: those SAME sentence(s) after the edit — the 'after'. Identical to `original` except for the intended change, so the redline diff is minimal and clean. The move is whatever is the BEST WRITING here per the research, not the shortest: reductive-mode lenses usually cut (de-AI tells, tics), constructive-mode lenses (scene/vivify/setting/tension/character/pacing/resonance…) prefer the additive or strengthening move the craft file names, including ENHANCEMENT additions (a telling object/gesture/sensory beat/withheld answer/interiority line woven into the surrounding sentences). ALWAYS: no em dash, never introduce a triad (land on two or four beats, never the matched three), never invent plot facts. Omit for structural.

action.description: STRUCTURAL ONLY: a ≤12-word concrete instruction for a change with no single-sentence diff ('lift the storm out of the montage into its own scene'). Omit for line edits.

illustration.description: STRUCTURAL ONLY, REQUIRED with `action`: a concrete 1-3 sentence example of what the action looks like, quoting the real manuscript spans it moves/cuts ('Pull “A storm came on the fourth night … It passed.” out of the montage and give it its own * * * scene'). The author must be able to picture the change without decoding the instruction. No em dash; if it invents plot, frame it as a possibility ('say William rations…'), never as the required fix.

anchor.description: a short locator quote (used to jump to the spot). For line edits this is usually the same as `original` or a slice of it.

route.description: where a QUEUED finding flows for the actual edit.

decision.description: AUTHOR-ONLY, set later on the /review page. Scanners NEVER set this.
